generatedSentence: Kael, a celebrated racer and brilliant inventor, had settled into a comfortable routine on the orbital rooftop gardens, far from the chaotic jungles of Neo-Kyoto. His mainframe was an intricate reticulum of graphics and sensor data, constantly upscaling his designs for next-gen turbojets. He enjoyed the chirping of synthetic orioles and the smell of exotic fruit and verbena that permeated his workshop. Life was enjoyable, and he felt insured against the usual political jinxing that plagued the minority factions. He even had a small anemonefish tank, a curious hedgehog that resembled a miniature kappa, and playful lapdogs for companionship. His work, often praiseworthy, included kitting out new operative gear. However, his peace was shattered when his protégé, a young musician, was kidnaped by ruthless mercenaries led by the notorious Flamer. Kael knew their itinerary involved a blacklisted data farm, where they planned to hacks into crucial networks. A sudden thunderbolt of realization hit him; this wasn't just a random act, it was a direct hit to his work. The pirates and rogues were clearly being manipulated by a larger, political entity. Kael felt a deep apathy towards their uninteresting motives, but his resolve solidified. He knew this was otherwise going to be a dirge for freedom, and he couldn't let it be. Donning his combat gear, Kael, an elite operative, felt a familiar jitter as he prepared to utter a defiant roar. He watched a cheetah-like drone mosey past on surveillance before unleashing his own advanced catapult drones, each equipped with miniature glaives. The horsepower of his modified trucks surged, as he flew towards the target, a fortified facility with a deceptive topography. He saw the juggernauts guarding the entrance and knew he had to carry the fight directly. The sight of the mercuries symbols on the bricks of the compound ignited his determination; he would secure his friend and victor over these villains. In a climactic struggle, Kael used a blender-like sonic device to confuse the guards, leaving them in negatives of their former selves. He found his protégé, unlatched from a makeshift waterpipe restraint, and quickly began to explain their escape route. A giant orca drone, a new model he had only just designed, provided cover as they made their return. They avoided the lynxes patrolling the perimeter and the buzzing bee-like reconnaissance bots. With the mission complete, Kael felt a sense of maturity. He knew this experience would change him, making him a more mature protector. The world seemed a little clearer, no longer just a pinprick of light in the vast darkness.
panelSentences: Kael, a celebrated racer and brilliant inventor, had settled into a comfortable routine on the orbital rooftop gardens, far from the chaotic jungles of Neo-Kyoto. His mainframe was an intricate reticulum of graphics and sensor data, constantly upscaling his designs for next-gen turbojets. He enjoyed the chirping of synthetic orioles and the smell of exotic fruit and verbena that permeated his workshop. Life was enjoyable, and he felt insured against the usual political jinxing that plagued the minority factions. He even had a small anemonefish tank, a curious hedgehog that resembled a miniature kappa, and playful lapdogs for companionship. His work, often praiseworthy, included kitting out new operative gear.

However, his peace was shattered when his protégé, a young musician, was kidnaped by ruthless mercenaries led by the notorious Flamer. Kael knew their itinerary involved a blacklisted data farm, where they planned to hacks into crucial networks. A sudden thunderbolt of realization hit him; this wasn't just a random act, it was a direct hit to his work. The pirates and rogues were clearly being manipulated by a larger, political entity. Kael felt a deep apathy towards their uninteresting motives, but his resolve solidified. He knew this was otherwise going to be a dirge for freedom, and he couldn't let it be.

Donning his combat gear, Kael, an elite operative, felt a familiar jitter as he prepared to utter a defiant roar. He watched a cheetah-like drone mosey past on surveillance before unleashing his own advanced catapult drones, each equipped with miniature glaives. The horsepower of his modified trucks surged, as he flew towards the target, a fortified facility with a deceptive topography. He saw the juggernauts guarding the entrance and knew he had to carry the fight directly. The sight of the mercuries symbols on the bricks of the compound ignited his determination; he would secure his friend and victor over these villains.

In a climactic struggle, Kael used a blender-like sonic device to confuse the guards, leaving them in negatives of their former selves. He found his protégé, unlatched from a makeshift waterpipe restraint, and quickly began to explain their escape route. A giant orca drone, a new model he had only just designed, provided cover as they made their return. They avoided the lynxes patrolling the perimeter and the buzzing bee-like reconnaissance bots. With the mission complete, Kael felt a sense of maturity. He knew this experience would change him, making him a more mature protector. The world seemed a little clearer, no longer just a pinprick of light in the vast darkness.

His **mainframe** was an intricate **reticulum** of **graphics** and sensor data, constantly **upscaling** his designs for next-gen **turbojets**. He enjoyed the **chirping** of synthetic **orioles** and the **smell** of exotic **fruit** and **verbena** that permeated his workshop. However, his peace was shattered when his protégé, a young **musician**, was **kidnaped** by ruthless **mercenaries** led by the notorious **Flamer**. Kael knew their **itinerary** involved a **blacklisted** data farm, where they planned to **hacks** into crucial networks. A sudden **thunderbolt** of realization hit him; this wasn't just a random act, it was a direct hit to his **work**. The **pirates** and **rogues** were clearly being manipulated by a larger, **political** entity. Kael felt a deep **apathy** towards their **uninteresting** motives, but his resolve solidified. Donning his combat gear, Kael, an elite **operative**, felt a familiar **jitter** as he prepared to **utter** a defiant **roar**. He watched a **cheetah**-like drone **mosey** past on surveillance before unleashing his own advanced **catapult** drones, each equipped with miniature **glaives**. The **horsepower** of his modified **trucks** surged, as he flew towards the target, a fortified facility with a deceptive **topography**. imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist: A determined figure wearing a custom-built chrome-plated racing suit, a glowing optical visor, and a utility belt equipped with miniature drone ports. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist Kael working diligently in a high-tech orbital rooftop garden, surrounded by glowing data screens and exotic flora, medium shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Protagonist looking up with a look of shock and resolve, a holographic news report showing a musician being captured by shadowy figures, close-up. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist in full action, piloting a sleek, futuristic vehicle through a cyberpunk cityscape towards a heavily fortified compound, dynamic low-angle shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Protagonist helping the freed musician escape the facility, as a massive orca-shaped drone creates a diversion, wide shot. Bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, no text, no speech bubbles, no dialogue.

definitions.definition: To seize and detain a person unlawfully; sometimes for ransom.
Someone who takes part in a race.
A racehorse.

A vehicle or other device used for racing.
Any of several genera of colubrid snakes.
A video game involving racing of any kind.

A large, powerful computer able to manage very many simultaneous tasks and communicate with very many connected terminals; used by large, complex organizations (such as banks and supermarkets) where continuously sustained operation is vital
One motivated by gain, especially monetary.
A person employed to fight in an armed conflict who is not a member of the state or military group for which they are fighting and whose primary motivation is private gain.
One hired to engage in a figurative battle, as a corporate takeover, a lawsuit, or a political campaign.

A mostly herbivorous insect of the order Orthoptera, noted for its ability to jump long distances and for the habit of some species communicating by stridulation; they are related to but distinct from crickets.
A cocktail made with crème de menthe and optionally with crème de cacao.
A young student in initial stages of training who has been chosen on account of their obvious talent.
In ordinary square or upright pianos of London make, the escapement lever or jack, so made that it can be taken out and replaced with the key.
A hex; an evil spell.
A person or thing supposed to bring bad luck.
To cast a spell on.
To bring bad luck to.
To cause something to happen by mentioning it, usually sarcastically.
Used after the same response is said by two people simultaneously.
A heavy breath; a grunt or sigh.
definitions.example: With a huff, he lifted the box onto the back of the truck.

definitions.definition: An expression of anger, annoyance, disgust, etc.
One swelled with a false sense of importance or value; a boaster.
To breathe heavily.
definitions.example: The run left him huffing and puffing.

definitions.definition: To say in a huffy manner.
To enlarge; to swell up.
definitions.example: Bread huffs.

definitions.definition: To bluster or swell with anger, arrogance, or pride; to storm; to take offense.
To treat with arrogance and insolence; to chide or rebuke rudely; to bully, to hector.
To inhale psychoactive inhalants.
(draughts) To remove an opponent's piece as a forfeit for deliberately not taking a piece (often signalled by blowing on it).
A machine outfitted with sharp blades, for mashing, crushing or liquefying food ingredients.
A piece of fabric sewn into the front of a theatrical wig to make it blend in with the performer's natural hair.
(quilting) A subtly patterned fabric printed in different shades of a single color, often used in place of a solid to create visual texture.
The Egyptian mongoose, Herpestes ichneumon, found in Africa and southern Europe.
The ichneumon wasp.
A hookah.
A pipe through which water flows, for example in a building.
An insignificant puncture made by a pin or similar point.
A mildly annoying wound or damage.
To produce a jabbing sensation, like a pinprick, in.

definitions.definition: To puncture with a tiny hole or holes.
Any of the clownfish, principally in the Amphiprion genus.
To make plain, manifest, or intelligible; to clear of obscurity; to illustrate the meaning of.

definitions.definition: To give a valid excuse for past behavior.
To make flat, smooth out.
To unfold or make visible.
To make something plain or intelligible.
Remove from a latch
The state of being a minor; youth, the period of a person's life prior to reaching adulthood.
Any subgroup that does not form a numerical majority.
definitions.example: Most people agreed, but a sizable minority were upset by the decision.

A member of an ethnic minority.

definitions.definition: A nervous action; a tic.
(chiefly in the plural, often with "the") A state of nervousness.

definitions.definition: An abrupt and unwanted variation of one or more signal characteristics.

: A rep performed with weight in which the muscle begins at maximum contraction and is slowly extended; a movement performed using only the eccentric phase of muscle movement.

To refuse; to veto.

To disprove.
To make ineffective; to neutralize.

definitions.definition: (intellectual property) To create and/or sell an unauthorized copy of

definitions.definition: To establish or become established in a steady position:
To fix one's residence in a place; to establish a dwelling place, home, or colony. (Compare settle down.)

definitions.definition: To move (people) to (a land or territory), so as to colonize it; to cause (people) to take residence in (a place).

definitions.definition: (often in the plural) In general, a product of plant growth useful to man or animals.

Specifically, a sweet, edible part of a plant that resembles seed-bearing fruit (see next sense), even if it does not develop from a floral ovary; also used in a technically imprecise sense for some sweet or sweetish vegetables, such as the petioles of rhubarb, that resemble a true fruit or are used in cookery as if they were a fruit.
A product of fertilization in a plant, specifically:
An end result, effect, or consequence; advantageous or disadvantageous result.
definitions.example: His long nights in the office eventually bore fruit when his business boomed and he was given a raise.

definitions.definition: Of, belonging to, related to, or having fruit or its characteristics; (of living things) producing or consuming fruit.

definitions.definition: To produce fruit, seeds, or spores.
A nightgown; female attire designed to be worn to bed.
definitions.example: She was too modest to come downstairs dressed only in her nightdress.

definitions.definition: (usually in phrases such as 'from the off', 'at the off', etc.) Beginning; starting point.
definitions.example: He has been very obviously an untrustworthy narrator right from the off.

definitions.definition: To kill.

definitions.definition: To switch off.

definitions.definition: Inoperative, disabled.

definitions.definition: Cancelled; not happening.

definitions.definition: Not fitted; not being worn.
definitions.example: The drink spilled out of the bottle because the top was off.

definitions.definition: Rancid, rotten, gone bad.
definitions.example: This milk is off!

definitions.definition: Less than normal, in temperament or in result.
definitions.example: sales are off this quarter

definitions.definition: Inappropriate; untoward.
definitions.example: I felt that his comments were a bit off.

definitions.definition: (in phrases such as 'well off', 'better off', 'poorly off') Circumstanced.
Started on the way.
definitions.example: And they're off! Whatsmyname takes an early lead, with Remember The Mane behind by a nose.

definitions.definition: Far; off to the side.
definitions.example: He took me down the corridor and into an off room.

definitions.definition: Designating a time when one is not strictly attentive to business or affairs, or is absent from a post, and, hence, a time when affairs are not urgent.
definitions.example: He took an off day for fishing.  an off year in politics; the off season

definitions.definition: (in phrases such as 'off day') Designating a time when one is not performing to the best of one's abilities.
(of a dish on a menu) Presently unavailable.
definitions.example: — I'll have the chicken please.

definitions.definition: (in relation to a vehicle) On the side furthest from the kerb (the right-hand side if one drives on the left).
definitions.example: The off front wheel came loose.

definitions.definition: In, or towards the half of the field away from the batsman's legs; the right side for a right-handed batsman.
In a direction away from the speaker or object.
definitions.example: He drove off in a cloud of smoke.

definitions.definition: Into a state of non-operation or non-existence.
definitions.example: Please switch off the light when you leave.

definitions.definition: So as to remove or separate, or be removed or separated.
definitions.example: He bit off the end of the carrot.

definitions.definition: Used in various other ways specific to individual idiomatic phrases, e.g. bring off, show off, put off, tell off, etc. See the entry for the individual phrase.
Not positioned upon; away from a position upon.
definitions.example: He's off the roof now.

definitions.definition: Detached, separated, excluded or disconnected from; away from a position of attachment or connection to.
definitions.example: He was thrown off the team for cheating.

definitions.definition: Used to indicate the location or direction of one thing relative to another, implying adjacency or accessibility via.
definitions.example: His office is off this corridor on the right.

definitions.definition: Used to express location at sea relative to land or mainland.
definitions.example: The island is 23 miles off the cape.

definitions.definition: Removed or subtracted from.
definitions.example: There's 20% off the list price.

definitions.definition: No longer wanting or taking.
definitions.example: He's been off his feed since Tuesday.

definitions.definition: (more properly 'from') Out of the possession of.
definitions.example: He didn't buy it off him. He stole it off him.

definitions.definition: Placed after a number (of products or parts, as if a unit), in commerce or engineering.
definitions.example: I'd like to re-order those printer cartridges, let's say 5-off.

definitions.definition: A flying insect, of the clade Anthophila within the hymenopteran superfamily Apoidea, known for its organised societies (though only a minority have them), for collecting pollen and (in some species) producing wax and honey.

Slingshot
An instance of firing a missile from a catapult.
An instance of firing something, as if from a catapult.
To fire a missile from a catapult.
To fire or launch something, as if from a catapult.
To increase the status of something rapidly.
definitions.example: The candidate selection for running mate has catapulted her to the national scene.

definitions.definition: To be fired from a catapult or as if from a catapult.
To have one's status increased rapidly.
definitions.example: She catapulted to the national scene following her selection by the candidate.

definitions.definition: To make a short, sharp, cheerful note, as of small birds or crickets
To speak in a high-pitched staccato
(radar, sonar, radio telescopy etc.) To modify (a pulse of signal) so that it sweeps through a band of frequencies throughout its duration.
To cheer up; to make (someone) happier.

A distinctive member (Acinonyx jubatus) of the cat family, slightly smaller than the leopard, but with proportionately longer limbs and a smaller head. It is native to Africa and also credited with being the fastest terrestrial animal.

Sustained effort to achieve a goal or result, especially overcoming obstacles.
definitions.example: We don't have much time. Let's get to work piling up those sandbags.

definitions.definition: (heading) Product; the result of effort.
The staging of events to appear as real.
Ore before it is dressed.
The equipment needed to inject a drug (syringes, needles, swabs etc.)
definitions.example: Tell me you're using clean works at least.

definitions.definition: Any reptile of the order Squamata that is not a snake, usually having four legs, external ear openings, movable eyelids and a long slender body and tail.
(chiefly in attributive use) Lizard skin, the skin of these reptiles.
An unctuous person.
A coward.

(rock paper scissors) A hand forming a "D" shape with the tips of the thumb and index finger touching (a handshape resembling a lizard), that beats paper and Spock and loses to rock and scissors in rock-paper-scissors-lizard-Spock.
(in compounds) A person who idly spends time in a specified place, especially a promiscuous female.
definitions.example: lounge lizard; lot lizard; beach lizard; truck stop lizard

definitions.definition: The act of determining, or the state of being determined.
definitions.example: Through sheer determination, we managed to escape from the tornado.

definitions.definition: Bringing to an end; termination; limit.
Direction or tendency to a certain end; impulsion.
The quality of mind which reaches definite conclusions; decision of character; resoluteness.
The state of decision; a judicial decision, or ending of controversy.
That which is determined upon; result of deliberation; purpose; conclusion formed; fixed resolution.
A flow, rush, or tendency to a particular part
definitions.example: a determination of blood to the head

definitions.definition: The act, process, or result of any accurate measurement, as of length, volume, weight, intensity, etc.
definitions.example: the determination of the level of salt in sea water

definitions.definition: The act of defining a concept or notion by giving its essential constituents.
The addition of a distinguishing feature to a concept or notion, thus limiting its extent; -- the opposite of generalization.
The act of determining the relations of an object, such as genus and species; the referring of minerals, plants, or animals, to the species to which they belong; classification
definitions.example: I am indebted to a friend for the determination of most of these shells.

definitions.definition: To assemble or collect something into kits or sets or to give somebody a kit. See also kit out and other derived phrases.
definitions.example: We need to kit the parts for the assembly by Friday, so that manufacturing can build the tool.

definitions.definition: (retail) The process of assembling or bundling various related goods for sale to increase revenue.

definitions.definition: Fully developed; grown up in terms of physical appearance, behaviour or thinking; ripe.

definitions.definition: Brought to a state of complete readiness.

definitions.definition: Profound; careful.

definitions.definition: Come to, or in a state of, completed suppuration.

To filter.

To spray with liquid from a container.

To leave.

definitions.example: This house is made of brick.

definitions.definition: Something shaped like a brick.
definitions.example: a plastic explosive brick

definitions.definition: A helpful and reliable person.
definitions.example: Thanks for helping me wash the car. You're a brick.

definitions.definition: A shot which misses, particularly one which bounces directly out of the basket because of a too-flat trajectory, as if the ball were a heavier object.
definitions.example: We can't win if we keep throwing up bricks from three-point land.

definitions.definition: A power brick; an external power supply consisting of a small box with an integral male power plug and an attached electric cord terminating in another power plug.
An electronic device, especially a heavy box-shaped one, that has become non-functional or obsolete.
A carton of 500 rimfire cartridges, which forms the approximate size and shape of a brick.
A community card (usually the turn or the river) which does not improve a player's hand.
definitions.example: The two of clubs was a complete brick on the river.

definitions.definition: The colour brick red.
One kilo of cocaine.
To build with bricks.
To make into bricks.
To hit someone or something with a brick.
To make an electronic device nonfunctional and usually beyond repair, essentially making it no more useful than a brick.
definitions.example: My VCR was bricked during the lightning storm.

definitions.definition: To pay attention to a sound or speech.
definitions.example: Please listen carefully as I explain.  I like to listen to music.

definitions.definition: To expect or wait for a sound, such as a signal.
definitions.example: You should listen for the starting gun.

definitions.definition: To accept advice or obey instruction; to agree or assent.
definitions.example: Listen, the only reason I yelled at you was because I was upset, OK?  Good children listen to their parents.

definitions.definition: To hear (something or someone), to pay attention to.
Of something or someone that listens.
definitions.example: Any listening person can tell she's lying.

definitions.definition: Of something that is used in order to hear or to improve the ability to hear.
definitions.example: He could hear better when he used his listening device.

definitions.definition: Of an action that is performed with caution and attention to sounds.
One who laughs.
A variety of the domestic pigeon.
A political agent or officer.
A publication focusing on politics.
Concerning or relating to politics, the art and process of governing.
definitions.example: Political principles are rarely absolute, as political logic holds an imperfect result by compromise is better than a theoretically perfect abstention from the political process in the opposition.

definitions.definition: Concerning a polity or its administrative components.
definitions.example: Good political staff is hard to find, they may neither be ambitious and corrupted by power nor tempted by private sector careers.

definitions.definition: Motivated, especially inappropriately, by political (electoral or other party political) calculation.
definitions.example: “The Court invalidates Minnesota’s political apparel ban based on its inability to define the term ‘political'”

definitions.definition: Of or relating to views about social relationships that involve power or authority.
(of a person) Interested in politics.
Payment, such as property or money, paid by the bride's family to the groom or his family at the time of marriage.
Payment by the groom or his family to the bride's family: bride price.
Dower.
A natural gift or talent.
To bestow a dowry upon.
definitions.example: 1999, Judith Everard, Michael C. E. Jones, Charters Duchess Constance Br, Page xvi

definitions.definition: A cocktail made with a measure of crème de cassis topped up with champagne.
A network.
A pattern of interconnected objects.
The second compartment of the stomach of a cow or other ruminant.
A long, loud, deep shout, as of rage or laughter, made with the mouth wide open.
The cry of the lion.
The deep cry of the bull.
A loud resounding noise.
definitions.example: the roar of a motorbike

definitions.definition: A show of strength or character.
To make a loud, deep cry, especially from pain, anger, or other strong emotion.
To laugh in a particularly loud manner.
definitions.example: The audience roared at his jokes.

definitions.definition: Of animals (especially the lion), to make a loud deep noise.
definitions.example: The lioness roared to scare off the hyenas.

definitions.definition: Generally, of inanimate objects etc., to make a loud resounding noise.
To proceed vigorously.
To cry aloud; to proclaim loudly.
To be boisterous; to be disorderly.
To make a loud noise in breathing, as horses do when they have a certain disease.
(North Midlands) to cry
A scoundrel, rascal or unprincipled, deceitful, and unreliable person.
A mischievous scamp.
A vagrant.
Deceitful software pretending to be anti-spyware, but in fact being malicious software itself.
An aggressive animal separate from the herd, especially an elephant.
A plant that shows some undesirable variation.
A character class focusing on stealthy conduct.
To cull; to destroy plants not meeting a required standard, especially when saving seed, rogue or unwanted plants are removed before pollination.
To cheat.
To give the name or designation of rogue to; to decry.
To wander; to play the vagabond; to play knavish tricks.
A sensation, pleasant or unpleasant, detected by inhaling air (or, the case of water-breathing animals, water) carrying airborne molecules of a substance.
definitions.example: I love the smell of fresh bread.

definitions.definition: The sense that detects odours.
A conclusion or intuition that a situation is wrong, more complex than it seems, or otherwise inappropriate.
To sense a smell or smells.
definitions.example: I can smell fresh bread.

definitions.definition: Followed by like or of if descriptive: to have a particular smell, whether good or bad.
definitions.example: Her feet smell of cheese.

definitions.definition: (without a modifier) To smell bad; to stink.
definitions.example: Ew, this stuff smells.

definitions.definition: To have a particular tincture or smack of any quality; to savour.
definitions.example: A report smells of calumny.

definitions.definition: To exercise sagacity.
To detect or perceive; often with out.
To give heed to.
A small wheel or roller, specifically the wheel of a gun carriage.
The ball on top of a flagpole.

On a wooden mast, a circular disc (or sometimes a rectangle) of wood near or at the top of the mast, usually with holes or sheaves to reeve signal halyards; also a temporary or emergency place for a lookout. "Main" refers to the mainmast, whereas a truck on another mast may be called (on the mizzenmast, for example) "mizzen-truck".
A semi-tractor ("semi") trailer; a lorry.
definitions.example: Mexican open-bed trucks haul most of the fresh produce that comes into the United States from Mexico.

definitions.definition: Any motor vehicle designed for carrying cargo, including delivery vans, pickups, and other motorized vehicles (including passenger autos) fitted with a bed designed to carry goods.
A garden cart, a two-wheeled wheelbarrow.
A small wagon or cart, of various designs, pushed or pulled by hand or pulled by an animal, as with those in hotels for moving luggage, or in libraries for transporting books.
A pantechnicon (removal van).
A flatbed railway car; a flatcar.

A pivoting frame, one attached to the bottom of the bed of a railway car at each end, that rests on the axle and which swivels to allow the axle (at each end of which is a solid wheel) to turn with curves in the track; a bogie.
The part of a skateboard or roller skate that joins the wheels to the deck, consisting of a hanger, baseplate, kingpin, and bushings, and sometimes mounted with a riser in between.
A platform with wheels or casters.
Dirt or other messiness.
To drive a truck: Generally a truck driver's slang.
To convey by truck.
definitions.example: Last week, Cletus trucked 100 pounds of lumber up to Dubuque.

definitions.definition: To travel or live contentedly.
definitions.example: Keep on trucking!

definitions.definition: To persist, to endure.
definitions.example: Keep on trucking!

definitions.definition: (film production) To move a camera parallel to the movement of the subject.
To fight or otherwise physically engage with.
To run over or through a tackler in American football.
To fail; run out; run short; be unavailable; diminish; abate.
To give in; give way; knuckle under; truckle.
To deceive; cheat; defraud.
To tread (down); stamp on; trample (down).
(often in the plural) Small, humble items; things, often for sale or barter.
The practice of paying workers in kind, or with tokens only exchangeable at a shop owned by the employer [forbidden in the 19th century by the Truck Acts].
Garden produce, groceries (see truck garden).
(usually with negative) Social intercourse; dealings, relationships.
To trade, exchange; barter.
To engage in commerce; to barter or deal.
To have dealings or social relationships with; to engage with.
Arousing little or no interest; boring or uneventful.
Outer; furthest out, most remote.
Outward.
Absolute, unconditional, total, complete.
definitions.example: utter ruin; utter darkness

definitions.definition: A goalkeeper or goaltender.
To act as a goalie, to tend the goals, to mind the net.
A large, undeveloped, humid forest, especially in a tropical region, that is home to many wild plants and animals; a tropical rainforest.
Any uncultivated tract of forest or scrub habitat.
A place where people behave ruthlessly, unconstrained by law or morality.
definitions.example: It’s a jungle out there.

definitions.definition: An area where hobos camp together.
A migrant camp.
A style of electronic music related to drum and bass.
(Israel) A desert region.
Dense rough.
A hairy vulva.
To cast a spell on.
To bring bad luck to.
To cause something to happen by mentioning it, usually sarcastically.
Verbena, a genus of herbaceous plants of which several species are extensively cultivated for the great beauty of their flowers; vervain.
A tool for chopping.
A hacking blow.
A gouge or notch made by such a blow.
A dry cough.
A hacking; a catch in speaking; a short, broken cough.
A try, an attempt.
The foothold traditionally cut into the ice from which the person who throws the rock pushes off for delivery.
A mattock or a miner's pickaxe.
An expedient, temporary solution, such as a small patch or change to code, meant to be replaced with a more elegant solution at a later date.
An interesting technical achievement, particularly in computer programming.
A trick, shortcut, skill, or novelty method to increase productivity, efficiency or ease.

definitions.definition: An illegal attempt to gain access to a computer network.
A video game or any computer software that has been altered from its original state.
Time check.
A swing of the bat at a pitched ball by the batter.
definitions.example: He took a few hacks, but the pitcher finally struck him out.

definitions.definition: A kick on the shins in football.
Confinement of an officer to their stateroom as a punishment.
To chop or cut down in a rough manner.
definitions.example: They hacked the brush down and made their way through the jungle.

definitions.definition: To cough noisily.
definitions.example: This cold is awful. I can't stop hacking.

definitions.definition: To withstand or put up with a difficult situation.
definitions.example: Can you hack it out here with no electricity or running water?

definitions.definition: To make a quick code change to patch a computer program, often one that, while being effective, is inelegant or makes the program harder to maintain.
definitions.example: I hacked in a fix for this bug, but we'll still have to do a real fix later.

definitions.definition: To accomplish a difficult programming task.
definitions.example: He can hack like no one else and make the program work as expected.

definitions.definition: To work with something on an intimately technical level.
definitions.example: I'm currently hacking distributed garbage collection.

definitions.definition: (by extension) To apply a trick, shortcut, skill, or novelty method to something to increase productivity, efficiency or ease.
definitions.example: I read up on dating tips so I can hack my sex life.

definitions.definition: To hack into; to gain unauthorized access to (a computer system, e.g., a website, or network) by manipulating code.
(by extension) To gain unauthorised access to a computer or online account belonging to (a person or organisation).
definitions.example: When I logged into the social network, I discovered I'd been hacked.

definitions.definition: To strike an opponent's leg with one's hockey stick.
definitions.example: He's going to the penalty box after hacking the defender in front of the goal.

definitions.definition: To make a flailing attempt to hit the puck with a hockey stick.
definitions.example: There's a scramble in front of the net as the forwards are hacking at the bouncing puck.

definitions.definition: To swing at a pitched ball.
definitions.example: He went to the batter's box hacking.

definitions.definition: (soccer and rugby) To kick (a player) on the shins.
To strike in a frantic movement.
To strike lightly as part of tapotement massage.
A board which the falcon's food is placed on; used by extension for the state of partial freedom in which they are kept before being trained.
A food-rack for cattle.
A rack used to dry something, such as bricks, fish, or cheese.
A grating in a mill race.
To lay (bricks) on a rack to dry.
To keep (young hawks) in a state of partial freedom, before they are trained.
A horse for hire, especially one which is old and tired.
A person, often a journalist, hired to do routine work.
definitions.example: I got by on hack work for years before I finally published my novel.

definitions.definition: Someone who is available for hire; hireling, mercenary.
A taxicab (hackney cab) driver.
A vehicle let for hire; originally, a hackney coach, now typically a taxicab.
A hearse.
(authorship) An untalented writer.
definitions.example: Dason is nothing but a two-bit hack.

definitions.definition: One who is professionally successful despite producing mediocre work. (Usually applied to persons in a creative field.)
A talented writer-for-hire, paid to put others' thoughts into felicitous language.
A political agitator. (slightly derogatory)
A writer who hires himself out for any sort of literary work; an overworked man; a drudge.
A procuress.
To make common or cliched; to vulgarise.
To ride a horse at a regular pace; to ride on a road (as opposed to riding cross-country etc.).
To be exposed or offered or to common use for hire; to turn prostitute.
To live the life of a drudge or hack.
To use as a hack; to let out for hire.
To use frequently and indiscriminately, so as to render trite and commonplace.
A small ball usually made of woven cotton or suede and filled with rice, sand or some other filler, for use in hackeysack.
To play hackeysack.
Power derived from the motion of a horse.
A non-metric unit of power (symbol hp) with various definitions, for different applications. The most common of them is probably the mechanical horsepower, approximately equal to 745.7 watts.
A metric unit (symbol often PS from the German abbreviation), approximately equal to 735.5 watts.
Strength.
definitions.example: political horsepower

definitions.definition: A manner of transporting or lifting something; the grip or position in which something is carried.
definitions.example: Adjust your carry from time to time so that you don't tire too quickly.

definitions.definition: A tract of land over which boats or goods are carried between two bodies of navigable water; a portage.
The bit or digit that is carried in an addition operation.
The benefit or cost of owning an asset over time.
definitions.example: The carry on this trade is 25 basis points per annum.

definitions.definition: The distance travelled by the ball when struck, until it hits the ground.
Carried interest.
The sky; cloud-drift.
To lift (something) and take it to another place; to transport (something) by lifting.
To transfer from one place (such as a country, book, or column) to another.
definitions.example: to carry an account to the ledger

definitions.definition: To convey by extension or continuance; to extend.
definitions.example: The builders are going to carry the chimney through the roof.  They would have carried the road ten miles further, but ran out of materials.

definitions.definition: To move; to convey using force
To lead or guide.
To stock or supply (something); to have in store.
definitions.example: The corner drugstore doesn't carry his favorite brand of aspirin.

definitions.definition: To adopt (something); take (something) over.
definitions.example: I think I can carry Smith's work while she is out.

definitions.definition: To adopt or resolve on, especially in a deliberative assembly
definitions.example: The court carries that motion.

definitions.definition: In an addition, to transfer the quantity in excess of what is countable in the units in a column to the column immediately to the left in order to be added there.
definitions.example: Five and nine are fourteen; carry the one to the tens place.

definitions.definition: To have, hold, possess or maintain (something).
definitions.example: Always carry sufficient insurance to protect against a loss.

definitions.definition: To be transmitted; to travel.
definitions.example: The sound of the bells carried for miles on the wind.

definitions.definition: To insult, to diss.
To capture a ship by coming alongside and boarding.
To transport (the ball) whilst maintaining possession.
To have on one's person.
definitions.example: she always carries a purse;  marsupials carry their young in a pouch

definitions.definition: To be pregnant (with).
definitions.example: The doctor said she's carrying twins.

definitions.definition: To have propulsive power; to propel.
definitions.example: A gun or mortar carries well.

definitions.definition: To hold the head; said of a horse.
definitions.example: to carry well, i.e. to hold the head high, with arching neck

definitions.definition: To have earth or frost stick to the feet when running, as a hare.
To bear or uphold successfully through conflict, for example a leader or principle
To succeed in (e.g. a contest); to succeed in; to win.
definitions.example: The Tories carried the election.

definitions.definition: To get possession of by force; to capture.
To contain; to comprise; have a particular aspect; to show or exhibit
To bear (oneself); to behave or conduct.
To bear the charges or burden of holding or having, as stocks, merchandise, etc., from one time to another.
definitions.example: A merchant is carrying a large stock;  a farm carries a mortgage;  a broker carries stock for a customer;  to carry a life insurance.

definitions.definition: To have a weapon on one's person; to be armed.
To be disproportionately responsible for a team's success.
definitions.example: He absolutely carried the game, to the point of killing the entire enemy team by himself.

definitions.definition: The process of becoming different.
definitions.example: The product is undergoing a change in order to improve it.

definitions.definition: Small denominations of money given in exchange for a larger denomination.
definitions.example: Can I get change for this $100 bill please?

definitions.definition: A replacement, e.g. a change of clothes
Balance of money returned from the sum paid after deducting the price of a purchase.
definitions.example: A customer who pays with a 10-pound note for a £9 item receives one pound in change.

definitions.definition: Usually coins (as opposed to paper money), but sometimes inclusive of paper money
definitions.example: Do you have any change on you? I need to make a phone call.

definitions.definition: A transfer between vehicles.
definitions.example: The train journey from Bristol to Nottingham includes a change at Birmingham.

definitions.definition: A change-up pitch.
(campanology) Any order in which a number of bells are struck, other than that of the diatonic scale.
A place where merchants and others meet to transact business; an exchange.
A public house; an alehouse.
To become something different.

definitions.definition: To make something into something else.
definitions.example: The fairy changed the frog into a prince.   I had to change the wording of the ad so it would fit.

definitions.definition: To replace.
definitions.example: Ask the janitor to come and change the lightbulb.   After a brisk walk, I washed up and changed my shirt.

definitions.definition: To replace one's clothing.
definitions.example: You can't go into the dressing room while she's changing.   The clowns changed into their costumes before the circus started.

definitions.definition: To replace the clothing of (the one wearing it).
definitions.example: It's your turn to change the baby.

definitions.definition: To transfer to another vehicle (train, bus, etc.)
To exchange.
To change hand while riding (a horse).

definitions.definition: A written schedule of activities for a vacation or road trip.
A route or proposed route of a journey.
An account or record of a journey.
A guidebook for travellers.
Itinerant; travelling from place to place; done on a journey
Any of several medium-sized wild cats, mostly of the genus Lynx.
The act of returning.

definitions.definition: A return ticket.
definitions.example: Do you want a one-way or a return?

definitions.definition: An item that is returned, e.g. due to a defect, or the act of returning it.
definitions.example: Last year there were 250 returns of this product, an improvement on the 500 returns the year before.

definitions.definition: An answer.
definitions.example: a return to one's question

definitions.definition: An account, or formal report, of an action performed, of a duty discharged, of facts or statistics, etc.; especially, in the plural, a set of tabulated statistics prepared for general information.
definitions.example: election returns; a return of the amount of goods produced or sold

definitions.definition: Gain or loss from an investment.
definitions.example: It yielded a return of 5%.

definitions.definition: : A report of income submitted to a government for purposes of specifying exact tax payment amounts. A tax return.
definitions.example: Hand in your return by the end of the tax year.

definitions.definition: A carriage return character.
The act of relinquishing control to the calling procedure.
A return value: the data passed back from a called procedure.
A return pipe, returning fluid to a boiler or other central plant (compare with flow pipe, which carries liquid away from central plant).
definitions.example: The boiler technician had to cut out the heating return to access the safety valve.

definitions.definition: A short perpendicular extension of a desk, usually slightly lower.
Catching a ball after a punt and running it back towards the opposing team.
A throw from a fielder to the wicket-keeper or to another fielder at the wicket.

The continuation in a different direction, most often at a right angle, of a building, face of a building, or any member, such as a moulding; applied to the shorter in contradistinction to the longer.
definitions.example: A facade of sixty feet east and west has a return of twenty feet north and south.

definitions.definition: To come or go back (to a place or person).
definitions.example: Although the birds fly north for the summer, they return here in winter.

definitions.definition: To go back in thought, narration, or argument.
definitions.example: To return to my story [...]

definitions.definition: To turn back, retreat.
To turn (something) round.
To place or put back something where it had been.

definitions.definition: To give something back to its original holder or owner.
definitions.example: You should return the library book within one month.

definitions.definition: To take back something to a vendor for a refund.
definitions.example: If the goods don't work, you can return them.

definitions.definition: To give in requital or recompense; to requite.
To bat the ball back over the net in response to a serve.
definitions.example: The player couldn't return the serve because it was so fast.

definitions.definition: To play a card as a result of another player's lead.
definitions.example: If one players plays a trump, the others must return a trump.

definitions.definition: To throw a ball back to the wicket-keeper (or a fielder at that position) from somewhere in the field.
To say in reply; to respond.

definitions.definition: To relinquish control to the calling procedure.
To pass (data) back to the calling procedure.

definitions.definition: To report, or bring back and make known.

definitions.definition: (by extension) To elect according to the official report of the election officers.
An elongated piece of wood or similar material, typically put to some use, for example as a wand or baton.
Any roughly cylindrical (or rectangular) unit of a substance.

definitions.definition: Material or objects attached to a stick or the like.
A tool, control, or instrument shaped somewhat like a stick.
A stick-like item:
Ability; specifically:

Encouragement or punishment, or (resulting) vigour or other improved behavior.
A measure.

Lack of emotion or motivation; lack of interest or enthusiasm towards something; disinterest (in something).

A metal.

A composer, conductor, or performer of music; specifically, a person who sings and/or plays a musical instrument as a hobby, an occupation, or a profession.
To give birth to a fawn.
To exhibit affection or attempt to please.
To seek favour by flattery and obsequious behaviour (with on or upon).

Servile flattery.
Seeking favor by way of flattery; flattering, servile.
A literal or metaphorical force or object regarded as unstoppable, that will crush all in its path.

An institution that incites destructive devotion or to which people are carelessly sacrificed.
A region of space that contains no matter.
(plural only "vacuums") A vacuum cleaner.
The condition of rarefaction, or reduction of pressure below that of the atmosphere, in a vessel, such as the condenser of a steam engine, which is nearly exhausted of air or steam, etc.

To use a vacuum cleaner.
To optimise a database or database table by physically removing deleted tuples.
The winner in a fight or contest.
The letter V in the ICAO spelling alphabet.
An employee or other worker with some particular function or skill.
A spy, secret agent, or detective.
A participant in an operation.
Effectual or important.
definitions.example: He's usually in a good mood — the operative word there being "usually". Today was a disaster.

definitions.definition: Functional, in working order.
Having the power of acting; hence, exerting force, physical or moral; active in the production of effects.

definitions.definition: Producing the appropriate or designed effect; efficacious.
definitions.example: an operative dose, rule, or penalty

definitions.definition: Based upon, or consisting of, a surgical operation or operations.

definitions.definition: A precious stone, especially an orient pearl.
A member or descendant of the peoples and cultures of the Orient.
A lily cultivar of a widely varied group, with strong scent.
A person from the eastern region of a place.
Of a pearl or other precious stone: having a superior lustre.
Pertaining to the eastern part of the sky; happening before sunrise.
Happening in the eastern part of a given place or location.
Pertaining to the regions east of the Mediterranean, beyond the Roman Empire or the early Christian world; of the Near East, the Middle East or the Far East, now especially relating to East Asia.
Designating various types of aromatic tobacco grown in Turkey and the Balkans.
Other than supposed; different.

definitions.definition: (manner) Differently, in another way.

definitions.definition: In different circumstances; or else.
definitions.example: I’m not well today, otherwise I would have helped.  You have to open your umbrella, otherwise you'll get wet.

definitions.definition: In all other respects.

Any of several spiny mammals, such as the porcupine, that are similar to the hedgehog.
A type of moveable military barricade made from crossed logs or steel bars, laced with barbed wire, used to damage or impede tanks and vehicles; Czech hedgehog.

To array with spiky projections like the quills of a hedgehog.

A particular style of walking associated with urban street culture.
(gerund) The action of the verb bowl.
Road bowling.
To make a pledge to (someone); to promise, guarantee (someone of something); to assure.
To make sure or certain of something (usually some future event or condition).
definitions.example: I use an alarm clock to ensure that I get up on time.

definitions.definition: To provide for compensation if some specified risk occurs. Often agreed by policy (contract) to offer financial compensation in case of an accident, theft or other undesirable event.
definitions.example: I'm not insured against burglary.

definitions.definition: To deal in such contracts; subscribe to a policy of insurance
The person or persons whose interests are protected by an insurance policy.
Covered by an insurance policy.

A small toy dog, kept as household pet, whose light weight and companionable temperament make it both suited and disposed to spend time resting in the comfort of its master's lap; a dog bred to behave in this manner.
(by extension) A person who behaves in a servile manner, such as a sycophantic employee or a fawning lover.
Meriting praise; worthy of high praise
A European bird, Corvus frugilegus, of the crow family.
A cheat or swindler; someone who betrays.
A type of firecracker used by farmers to scare birds of the same name.
A trick-taking game, usually played with a specialized deck of cards.
A bad deal, a rip-off.
To cheat or swindle.
(somewhat formal) The area atop a roof.
The top layer of a roof; the material covering or composing a roof.
First-rate; of the highest quality; exceptionally good.
definitions.example: This champagne is superb.

definitions.definition: Grand; magnificent; august; stately.
definitions.example: a superb edifice;  a superb colonnade

definitions.definition: Haughty.
To increase in size, to scale up.
A mythical beast having the body of a lion and the wings and head of an eagle.
A large vulture (Gyps fulvus) found in the mountainous parts of Southern Europe, North Africa, and Asia Minor, supposed to be the "eagle" of the Bible.
An English variety of apple.
A person who has just arrived from Europe.
A cadet newly arrived in British India: half English, half Indian.
A watchful guardian, especially a duenna in charge of a young woman.
A light lance with a long, sharp-pointed head.
A weapon consisting of a pole with a large blade fixed on the end, the edge of which is on the outside curve.
A sword, particularly a broadsword.
A person who catches fish, especially for a living or for sport.
A person attempting to catch fish.
A single tortilla chip from a dish of nachos.
